Name: information_theoretic_compression
Title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Deep Learning Model Compression
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of model compression in deep learning can be characterized using information-theoretic tools, and that these limits can be used to develop more efficient and effective model compression methods.
Related Work: Recent works, such as EMQ and AMP-ViT, have explored various aspects of model compression, including mixed-precision quantization and adaptive bit-width allocation. However, these works have not explicitly addressed the information-theoretic limits of model compression. Our proposal seeks to fill this gap by providing a rigorous theoretical framework for understanding the fundamental limits of model compression.
Abstract: Model compression is a crucial technique for deploying deep learning models in resource-constrained environments. However, the fundamental limits of model compression are not well understood. In this proposal, we aim to explore the information-theoretic limits of model compression in deep learning using tools from rate-distortion theory and statistical learning theory. We will develop a theoretical framework for characterizing the trade-off between model complexity and compression ratio, and investigate the role of distortion measures in determining the optimal compression strategy.
Experiments: Develop a rate-distortion theory-based framework for characterizing the trade-off between model complexity and compression ratio
Investigate the role of distortion measures in determining the optimal compression strategy
Evaluate the performance of our compression method on various deep learning models and datasets
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the theoretical framework may require significant mathematical derivations and proofs
The experimental evaluation of our compression method may require significant computational resources and large datasets
